     The Numbers Business Report

(500) Days of Summer (2009)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $32,425,665Details
International Box Office $2,089,638
Worldwide Box Office $34,515,303
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $16,248,733
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,145,141
Total $20,393,874
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Tom, greeting-card writer and hopeless romantic, is caught completely off-guard when his girlfriend, Summer, suddenly dumps him. He reflects on their 500 days together to try to figure out where their love affair went sour, and in doing so, Tom rediscovers his true passions in life.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$834,501 (2.6% of total gross)
Legs:8.67 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:93.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$7,500,000 (worldwide box office is 4.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:27 opening theaters/1,048 max. theaters, 7.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $51,708,127

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 17th, 2009 (Limited) by Fox Searchlight
August 7th, 2009 (Expands Wide) by Fox Searchlight
International Releases: September 17th, 2009 (Limited) (Australia)
January 21st, 2010 (Limited), released as 500Days Of Summer (South Korea)
Video Release: December 22nd, 2009 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sexual material and language
Running Time: 95 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Breaking the Fourth Wall, Split Screen
Plot point: Architecture, Non-Chronological, Relationships Gone Wrong, Romance, Unreliable Narrator
Subgenre: Romantic Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Romantic Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Fox Searchlight Pictures, Watermark Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Tom, greeting-card writer and hopeless romantic, is caught completely off-guard when his girlfriend, Summer, suddenly dumps him. He reflects on their 500 days together to try to figure out where their love affair went sour, and in doing so, Tom rediscovers his true passions in life.
